abstract: We propose a novel approach to human pose estimation centered on Deep Neural Networks (DNNs). By formulating pose estimation as a direct DNN-based regression problem, we enable the model to predict the precise Cartesian coordinates of body joints. To achieve high localization accuracy, we introduce a cascade of these DNN regressors that iteratively refines joint predictions. A key advantage of this framework is its ability to reason about body pose in a holistic, non-linear fashion, capturing complex global context that traditional part-based models often miss. Despite its simple formulation, our method capitalizes on recent breakthroughs in deep learning to eliminate the need for manually designed features or explicit graphical models. We provide a comprehensive empirical evaluation demonstrating that our approach achieves state-of-the-art performance across four challenging academic benchmarks featuring diverse, real-world imagery.
